lever: the Rust is_connected BFS (fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs:8722) scanned ALL O(|E|) edges even after every node was visited — no early exit. nx's _plain_bfs stops at `len(seen) == len(G)`. Add `if visited_count == n { break }` after marking a node visited: a connected graph is proven connected once all nodes are reached, so a dense/low-diameter graph costs O(|V|) not O(|E|).

parity.coverage: connected (complete/cycle/path/ba/ws), disconnected (disjoint unions, multi-component), single-node selfloop, empty(raise), 25 random gnp

headline: is_connected dense: native BFS lacked early-exit, O(|E|) -> 4.40x slower than nx on K601. Add early-exit -> 16x FASTER than nx (63x self-speedup), byte-exact. Broadly benefits every is_connected caller (is_eulerian K601 residual 3.14x -> 1.80x). Rust kernel change.
followup: connected_components / number_connected_components may have the same missing early-exit for the connected-graph fast path; is_eulerian undirected residual now is the DegreeView O(V) cost.
